p.seg: John, by the grace of God Bishop of Whithorn, to all sons and faithful of Holy Mother Church, Greeting:

Let present and future come to know that with the agreement of the whole of our chapter, for the salvation of our soul and of all predecessors’ and successors’, we have granted and by episcopal authority confirmed by the testimony of the present document, to God and the church of Holyrood at Edinburgh, and to the canons serving God there now and in the future, in free and pure and perpetual alms, all the churches, with their lands, that Fergus of good memory, and Uhtred his son, and David son of Terrus, bestowed to the same canons, inspired by divine reward, with the agreement of the diocesan bishiop, and made firm with their charters. That is, from the donation of Fergus, the church of Dunrod with the whole toun and with all its pertinents, and the Isle of Trail, and Galtway with the church of the same toun and with all its pertinents. And from the donation of Uhtred his son, the church of St Cuthbert of Desnesmor, and the church of Tongland, and the church of Twynholm, and the church of St Andrew, and the church of Kelton, and the church of Kirkcormack with the chapel of Barncrosh. And from the donation of David son of Terrus, the church of Anwoth with the chapel of Culenes, and with all its pertinents. Wherefore we will and grant by episcopal authority that the aforesaid canons should wholly convert to their own benefit all the aforenamed churches with chapels and all their revenues, and with lands and all their pertinents, as it pleases them; and hold and possess them freely and quietly and released from all procuration and exaction in perpetuity; and they should cause the same chapels to be served by chaplains agreeable to them, presented by us or our successors, or by themselves, as it seems expedient to them, and they may remove the less suitable ones in consultation with the bishop. Reserving to the ourself and our successors only reasonable synodals, and common aids from the aforesaid churches, except from the churches of Kirkcormack, with the chapel of Barncrosh, and Kelton, which have been entirely exempted from synodals and the rest of the episcopal dues by our predecessor, Bishop Christian of good memory, and by us.

With these witnesses, Michael the prior of Whithorn, and the chapter of the same place, the lord Alan son of Roland, constable of the lord king of Scots, John the archdeacon, Matthew the dean of Desnes, William the dean of Farines, Gilbert the dean of Rhinns, Walter the parson of Kinkandres, Durand the parson of Minnigaff, Martin the clerk of Kirkcudbright, and very many others.
